name: Western-Style Peking Duck
recipeInstructions:
1. Rinse the duck inside and out, pat dry. Remove tail and discard it. Reserve the giblets for another use.
2. Mix together ginger, cinnamon, nutmeg, pepper, and cloves. Sprinkle half a teaspoon of this spice mixture into the duck cavity.
3. Stir 1 tablespoon of soy sauce into the remaining spice mixture, then rub evenly over the exterior of the bird.
4. Cut one green onion in half and tuck it inside the duck cavity. Cover and refrigerate for 2 hours or until the next day.
5. Set the duck, breast side up, on a rack over boiling water (1.5 to 2 inches deep). Cover and steam for one hour, adding more water if necessary as it evaporates.
6. Cool the duck slightly, then drain and discard juices and green onion from the cavity. Place the duck, breast side up, on a rack in a baking pan and prick the skin all over with a fork.
7. Bake at 375 degrees for 30 minutes.
8. Blend remaining 2 tablespoons of soy sauce with honey and brush onto the duck.
9. Turn the oven temperature to 500 degrees. Bake for 5 minutes or until the skin becomes richly browned; do not allow the skin to char.
10. While the duck is roasting, cut the remaining green onions and their tops into 1.5-inch pieces, then slice them lengthwise into thin strips. Serve the green onions and plum sauce in separate bowls.
11. When the duck is roasted, remove the skin and cut it into roughly 2-inch square pieces. Slice the meat into bite-size pieces. Reserve the bones for duck soup.
12. Arrange the skin and duck pieces on a serving plate and garnish with cilantro.
13. To eat, place a small piece of skin and meat on a Mandarin pancake. Top with a few green onion slivers and a dab of plum sauce, then fold the panc
